meta.title: OB/GYN Practice Software | Prenatal RTM & Patient Flow Optimization | clinIQ
meta.description: Complete guide to OB/GYN practice operations. $7,800/month in prenatal/postpartum RTM. Mixed OB and GYN scheduling. Ultrasound coordination. Labor triage visibility.

hero.subhead: Your prenatal patients already report symptoms between visits — that's billable RTM. Your mixed OB/GYN schedule needs optimization. Your ultrasounds need coordination. clinIQ captures prenatal RTM, manages complex scheduling, and coordinates obstetric care.

sections.content:
OB/GYN practices run complex operations mixing obstetric care with gynecologic services.

**The prenatal monitoring opportunity:**

Prenatal patients should track: blood pressure, weight, fetal movement, symptoms (contractions, bleeding, headache, visual changes). This monitoring between visits identifies complications early.

60 prenatal patients × $130/month × ~8 months average = significant RTM revenue.

**The scheduling complexity:**

OB patients need frequent visits (monthly, then biweekly, then weekly). GYN patients need various visit types (annuals, procedures, post-ops). Ultrasounds need room and sonographer coordination. The schedule is inherently complex.

**The ultrasound coordination problem:**

OB ultrasounds (dating, anatomy, growth) and GYN ultrasounds (pelvic, follicle monitoring) need scheduling, completion, and review coordination.

**The postpartum gap:**

Postpartum patients should be monitored but often aren't seen until 6 weeks. RTM can maintain connection during this critical period.

Prenatal care is perfectly suited for RTM. Between-visit monitoring is standard of care.

**What prenatal patients track:**

Vital signs:
- Blood pressure (hypertension detection)
- Weight (excessive gain or loss)
- Fetal kick counts (after 28 weeks)

Symptoms:
- Contractions (frequency, regularity)
- Bleeding or spotting
- Headache or visual changes (preeclampsia warning)
- Swelling (edema patterns)
- Decreased fetal movement

Compliance:
- Prenatal vitamin use
- Gestational diabetes monitoring (if applicable)
- Activity restrictions (if indicated)

**The CPT codes:**

98975: RTM setup — one-time. ~$20-25.
98977: RTM device supply. ~$50-60/month.
98980: RTM treatment management — first 20 minutes. ~$50-70/month.

Typical: $120-150/patient/month.

**What clinIQ does for prenatal RTM:**

Gestational-age-appropriate prompts: First trimester symptoms differ from third trimester.

Urgent symptom alerts: Bleeding, decreased fetal movement, preeclampsia symptoms → immediate alerts.

BP tracking: Home BP integrated into prenatal record.

Kick count tracking: After 28 weeks, simplified kick count logging.

**The clinical value:**

Early detection of preeclampsia, preterm labor, and fetal compromise improves outcomes and reduces emergent interventions.

OB/GYN scheduling is inherently complex.

**Visit type variation:**

OB visits:
- New OB: 45-60 minutes
- Routine prenatal: 15-20 minutes
- High-risk prenatal: 30-45 minutes
- NST: 30-60 minutes
- Postpartum: 20-30 minutes

GYN visits:
- Annual exam: 20-30 minutes
- Problem visit: 15-20 minutes
- Procedure (IUD, colposcopy): 30-45 minutes
- Post-op: 15-20 minutes

**The OB scheduling constraint:**

OB patients need to see their provider (or covering provider). Visit frequency increases as pregnancy progresses. Labor and delivery pulls providers away unpredictably.

**What clinIQ does:**

Visit type awareness: Different visit types have different durations and room requirements.

Provider panel visibility: Which OB patients belong to which provider? Coverage protocols.

Urgent visit handling: Triage visits that need same-day attention.

Labor coverage: When provider is at L&D, schedule adjustments visible.

OB/GYN practices run significant ultrasound volume.

**OB ultrasounds:**
- Dating (first trimester)
- NT scan (11-14 weeks)
- Anatomy scan (18-22 weeks)
- Growth scans (third trimester)
- BPP, AFI (surveillance)

**GYN ultrasounds:**
- Pelvic US for symptoms
- Follicle monitoring (fertility)
- SIS/HSG coordination

**What clinIQ does:**

US scheduling: Room and sonographer availability coordinated.

US tracking: Ordered → Scheduled → Completed → Read → Results communicated.

Prenatal US timeline: Appropriate gestational age for each US type.

Results integration: US results visible in prenatal record.

The postpartum period is a care gap in traditional OB care.

**Traditional model:**

Delivery → 6-week postpartum visit. That's 6 weeks of no contact during a high-risk period (postpartum depression, infection, hemorrhage, hypertension).

**RTM-supported model:**

Delivery → RTM enrollment → Daily/weekly symptom tracking → Issues detected early → 6-week visit for comprehensive assessment.

**What postpartum patients track:**

Mood: PHQ-2/EPDS screening for postpartum depression

Physical recovery:
- Bleeding (quantity, duration)
- Incision healing (if cesarean)
- Pain level
- Fever or signs of infection

Breastfeeding:
- Breast symptoms (engorgement, mastitis)
- Feeding concerns

**The RTM value:**

Postpartum RTM can bill for 4-6 weeks after delivery. Patients stay connected. Depression and complications detected early.

**Week 1: Configuration**

Prenatal and postpartum RTM templates. OB/GYN visit types. US coordination workflow.

**Week 2: Training and Go-Live**

Clinical staff learns RTM enrollment and monitoring. Schedulers learn mixed schedule management.

**ROI Timeline:**

Month 1: Scheduling visibility immediate. RTM enrollment starting.

Month 3: RTM billing at scale. US coordination improving.

Month 6: Full RTM revenue. Prenatal care optimized.

**ROI Math:**

RTM revenue: 60 prenatal patients × $130/month × 6 months average = $46,800/year ongoing

With turnover: ~$7,800/month as patients deliver and new patients enroll

Postpartum RTM adds additional revenue during recovery period.

**Investment:**

Professional: $499/month

Implementation: $750 one-time

faqs.q: Can you bill RTM throughout pregnancy?
faqs.a: Yes. Prenatal RTM can bill monthly from enrollment through delivery. Postpartum RTM continues for 4-6 weeks after delivery.

faqs.q: How does RTM work with global OB billing?
faqs.a: RTM is separate from global OB care. The monitoring and treatment management are distinct services and can be billed in addition to global OB.
High-risk pregnancies benefit even more from RTM. More frequent monitoring, earlier detection of complications. Some may qualify for additional 98981 time.

faqs.q: Does this integrate with fetal monitoring?
faqs.a: Patient-reported fetal movement counts are captured. NST and BPP scheduling and results are tracked separately.

testimonial.quote: We caught two cases of developing preeclampsia from home BP monitoring. Both avoided emergency interventions.
